data.title: Visa slips for the seventh straight session
data.description:
Shares of Visa (V) slipped -0.81% to close at $306.45 on Thursday, marking a seventh straight session of losses.

The slide comes amid broader market weakness as escalating Middle East tensions and rising oil prices fueled concerns about consumer spending and travel demand.

Shares have declined about 3.6% between March 4 and March 11, compared with a roughly 1.4% drop in the S&P 500 over the same period, while peer Mastercard (MA) has fallen around 4.2% during that stretch.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, Visa is rated a Hold, with a score of 3.37 out of 5, receiving an A+ for profitability, but an F in terms of valuation.

However, on the Wall Street analysts are bullish, with 36 of 39 analysts rate the stock with a Buy or higher, and three recommending a Hold.

A Seeking Alpha analysis said both Visa and Mastercard continue to benefit from structural tailwinds including the global shift toward electronic payments, e-commerce growth and cross-border spending, even as broader economic slowdowns or weaker consumer activity could weigh on transaction volumes.

The note added that the companies operate highly scalable networks that profit from payment growth without taking on lending exposure, noting that “they collect a fee for facilitating the transaction, but they do not hold the credit risk. That risk remains on the balance sheet of the issuing bank.”

Shares have fallen around 5.6% in the past month and have slipped approximately 12% year-to-date.

data.title: Starbucks snaps six straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Starbucks (SBUX) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock closed 1.4% lower at $99.99 on Thursday.

The Seattle-based coffee chain gained over 4% in the preceding six sessions. Overall, the stock rose nearly 21% so far this year, compared to the 2% fall in the broader S&P 500 Index.

SBUX is up nearly 4% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.7% higher on Wednesday at $101.44.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, SBUX has a Hold rating with a score of 3.11 out of 5. The company received A in the prospect of momentum, while it got D- in the valuation factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 17 analysts gave SBUX a Buy and above, 17 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and four recommended Sell or lower. Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Seeking Alpha analyst Sanjay Chandiramani said Starbucks is downgraded to a sell as returns on tangible assets and earnings yield have deteriorated, with valuation now well above historical norms.

Another Seeking Alpha analyst Bela Lakos downgraded the stock from buy to hold due to deteriorating profitability and an uninspiring growth outlook.

Starbucks, in January, issued its first-quarter earnings report, where it recorded its second straight quarter of positive comparable sales after a streak of seven quarters in negative territory.

data.title: Brent crude tops $100 for the first time since 2022 as Middle East tensions shake markets
data.description:
Brent crude futures (CO1:COM) settled above $100 per barrel on Thursday, marking the first close above that level since August 2022 and underscoring mounting stress across global energy markets as tensions involving Iran intensify.

The milestone reflects a series of supply disruptions rippling through the Middle East, a region that remains central to global oil production and transportation. At the heart of the latest price surge is turmoil surrounding the Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime chokepoint through which roughly one-fifth of the world’s oil supply typically flows. Any threat to shipments through the narrow waterway can quickly tighten global supply expectations and drive prices sharply higher.

Additional disruptions across the region have compounded the pressure. Iraqi oil output has declined amid operational challenges, while Iranian drone strikes targeting major Saudi Arabian refining facilities have heightened concerns about the security of key energy infrastructure. Meanwhile, natural gas production stoppages in Qatar have further strained the broader energy complex.

Together, these developments have heightened fears of prolonged supply constraints, prompting traders to reprice risk in global energy markets. With geopolitical tensions showing little sign of easing, analysts warn that volatility in oil prices could remain elevated in the near term. 

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL). 

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), (AMLP), and (IXC).

data.title: Burning Rock Biotech GAAP EPS of -$0.02, revenue of $18.1M
data.description:
• Burning Rock Biotech press release (BNR): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.02.
  • Revenue of $18.1M.

  • Net loss was RMB15.4 million (US$2.2 million) for the three months ended December 31, 2025, compared to RMB81.3 million for the same period in 2024.

    Cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash were RMB481.1 million (US$68.8 million) as of December 31, 2025.

    Full Year 2025 Financial Results

    Revenues were RMB539.6 million (US$77.2 million) for 2025, representing a 4.6% increase from RMB515.8 million for 2024.

    • Revenue generated from central laboratory business was RMB160.0 million (US$22.9 million) for 2025, representing a 8.9% decrease from RMB175.6 million for 2024, primarily attributable to a decrease in the number of tests, as we continued our transition towards in-hospital testing and pharma research and development services.
    • Revenue generated from in-hospital business was RMB224.1 million (US$32.0 million) for 2025, remaining relatively stable as compared with RMB224.5 million for 2024.
    • Revenue generated from pharma research and development services was RMB155.5 million (US$22.2 million) for 2025, representing a 34.5% increase from RMB115.7 million for 2024, primarily attributable to an increased development and testing services performed for our pharma customers.

data.title: Blackline Safety Non-GAAP EPS of $0.02, revenue of $38.8M
data.description:
• Blackline Safety press release (BLN:CA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.02.
  • Revenue of $38.8M.

  • Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $1.7 million, a 12% improvement from $1.5 million in the prior year’s quarter. This marks the seventh consecutive quarter of positive Adjusted EBITDA, demonstrating the durability of Blackline’s profitability. Net loss for the quarter was $2.8 million compared to a loss of $1.1 million in the prior year’s quarter, largely driven by a foreign exchange loss in the quarter of $1.5 million compared to a gain in the prior year's quarter of $1.2 million in Q1 2025.

data.title: Traders no longer expect a full 25-bp Fed rate cut this year - report
data.description:
Traders are no longer fully pricing in a one-quarter point interest rate cut by the Federal Open Market Committee this year as climbing oil prices fuel inflation fears.

Interest rate swaps coinciding with Federal Reserve policy meeting dates are pricing in only 20 basis points of easing by the end of the year, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday. That compared with the expectation of ~30 bps of easing by the end of 2026 on Wednesday.

The FOMC holds its next meeting on Tuesday and Wednesday. Trading activity indicates a 99.2% probability that the Fed will hold its benchmark rate at 3.50%-3.75%, up from a 91.6% probability a month ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

For the last meeting of the year on Dec. 8-9, traders assign a 46.1% probability that the rate will still be where it is today, up from 23.5% on Wednesday. The likelihood of a 25-bp cut is 38.6%, according to the trading activity.

Concern that surging oil prices will trigger further inflation "has pushed the market to price out the Fed to just one cut, and currently just below one cut," John Briggs, head of US rates at Natixis SA’s investment banking unit, told Bloomberg.

Bond prices fell on Thursday, pushing up Treasury yields. The 10-year U.S. Treasury (US10Y) yield rose to 4.27%, up 4 basis points from Wednesday's close. Equity averages also slumped. The Nasdaq declined 1.5%, and the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average both slid 1.2% in late Thursday trading.

Earlier,  Goldman now sees first Fed rate cut in September, not June

data.title: BBB Foods outlines 29–32% revenue growth and up to 630 new stores in 2026 while accelerating store expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BBB Foods Inc. (TBBB) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Kamal Hatoum, Founder, CEO & Chairman, highlighted "another quarter of excellent performance and closed the year with strong momentum," noting that BBB Foods achieved rapid and disciplined store expansion, robust same-store sales, and solid cash generation. The company opened 184 net new stores in Q4 and 574 for the year, surpassing its guidance of 500 to 550 new stores. Two new distribution centers were added in the quarter, bringing the annual total to four.
  • Hatoum reported same-store sales growth of 16.6% in Q4 and 18.3% for the full year, with total revenues in Q4 increasing 34% to MXN 22 billion. For the full year, revenues reached MXN 78 billion, up 36% year-over-year.
  • The CEO underscored ongoing improvement in customer value proposition, higher initial sales from newer store cohorts, and increasing private label penetration, which represented 58% of merchandise sales in 2025 compared to 54% in 2024.
  • Eduardo Pizzuto, CFO & Investor Relations Officer, stated, "Sales [indiscernible] expenses as a percentage of revenue declined from 11.7% to 10.5% year-over-year in the fourth quarter of 2025." He explained the quarter included a one-time asset write-off of MXN 230 million related to the termination of a payment terminal provider. He noted, "we are pursuing all available legal actions in connection with this matter."

Outlook

  • The company expects same-store sales growth between 13% and 16%, 590 to 630 net new stores, and revenue growth between 29% and 32% for 2026, as outlined by CFO Pizzuto. He added that unit economics were updated, targeting a payback period of 26 months and a cash-on-cash return of roughly 55% by year three, with CapEx per store at approximately MXN 5.5 million. "These target unit economics are based on the performance trends we are currently observing in our newer stores."

Financial Results

  • Total revenue in Q4 rose to MXN 22 billion, matching a 34% year-over-year increase. For the full year, revenues reached MXN 78 billion, a 36% rise. Reported EBITDA for Q4 was MXN 79 million, but excluding noncash share-based compensation and the asset write-off, EBITDA was MXN 1.2 billion, with adjusted EBITDA margin declining 48 basis points year-over-year. For the full year, adjusted EBITDA was MXN 4.4 billion, up 30% versus 2024. Operating cash flow for the year reached MXN 4.7 billion, a nearly 25% increase year-over-year.
  • Negative working capital in December 2025 reached MXN 8.9 billion, representing 11.4% of total revenue, excluding IPO proceeds.

Q&A

  • Alvaro Garcia, Banco BTG Pactual S.A., asked about stock-based compensation and unit economics; CFO Pizzuto clarified that "what you're seeing in the numbers is what was granted in the -- all of 2025, so you should not expect an additional number for 2025," and added that updated unit economics do not assume incremental sales from higher CapEx per store.
  • Melissa Byun, BofA Securities, questioned ticket dynamics and product innovation. CEO Hatoum explained that "2/3 of the growth is explained by volume and 1/3 by average price," and highlighted ongoing product innovation, with about 60 new products being tested at any time.
  • Fernando Froylan Mendez Solther, JPMorgan Chase & Co, enquired about EBITDA and leasing costs, to which CFO Pizzuto pointed to the volume of Q4 store and distribution center openings as the main driver of higher lease payments, while also confirming a shift to larger store formats and more refrigeration equipment.
  • Irma Sgarz, Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., pressed on operating leverage and geographic build-out. CFO Pizzuto responded that "over the long run...admin expenses to decline as a percentage of sales," but hiring would continue to support growth. CEO Hatoum reiterated the consistent store expansion strategy and higher CapEx due to larger store formats.
  • Andrew Ruben, Morgan Stanley, asked about the high same-store sales in 2025. CEO Hatoum acknowledged the 18% result exceeded expectations and returned guidance to 15–16% as a realistic outlook.
  • Héctor Maya López, Scotiabank, discussed store format and payment processing. CEO Hatoum clarified store size is slightly larger due to real estate strategy, and refrigeration expansion is for more categories, not fresh produce. He stated, regarding payment processing, "No, not at all. We're even more competitive."
  • Antonio Hernandez, Actinver Casa de Bolsa, S.A. de C.V., asked about regional performance. CEO Hatoum noted "extremely consistent performance across all our regions."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were persistent on clarifying stock-based compensation, operating leverage, and sustainability of high growth rates, displaying a neutral to slightly positive tone, with focus on specifics and follow-ups for additional detail.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, reinforcing growth achievements and expansion plans. CEO Hatoum repeatedly expressed confidence in the company's model and outlook, using phrases such as "very confident in the long-term opportunity ahead."
  • The tone was slightly more positive in the current quarter compared to the previous quarter, with management emphasizing record store openings and maintaining robust guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is higher, with 590 to 630 net new stores projected versus the prior quarter's guidance of 500 to 550 for 2025. Revenue growth guidance for 2026 is set at 29–32%, compared to realized 36% growth in 2025.
  • Store expansion accelerated in Q4, with 184 net new stores versus 131 in Q3. Same-store sales growth moderated slightly to 16.6% in Q4 from 17.9% in Q3.
  • Management's tone shifted to highlight surpassing targets and addressing operational costs tied to expansion, while analysts maintained focus on clarification of cost structure and sustainability of growth.
  • Product innovation and private label share increased quarter-over-quarter, with private label rising to 58% of total merchandise sales from 54% in 2024.

Risks and Concerns

  • CFO Pizzuto disclosed a one-time asset write-off of MXN 230 million due to the termination of a payment terminal provider, with ongoing legal actions to recover the balance.
  • Increased CapEx per store is attributed to additional refrigeration and larger store formats, which could impact future margins if not offset by higher sales.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of high same-store sales growth, potential dilution from stock-based compensation, and the impact of increased leasing and administrative expenses.
  • Management emphasized conservative assumptions in unit economics and ongoing investments in talent and infrastructure to support growth.

Final Takeaway

BBB Foods closed 2025 with record store expansion, robust revenue growth, and higher private label participation, while addressing operational challenges from rapid scaling. The company projects continued strong performance in 2026, aiming for up to 630 new stores and 29–32% revenue growth, supported by disciplined expansion, operational improvements, and a consistent focus on enhancing the customer value proposition across all regions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BGSF outlines mid-single-digit sales growth for 2026 with PropTech market entry and cost reductions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BGSF, Inc. (BGSF) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Keith Schroeder, Co-CEO and CFO, described 2025 as "a transformational year for the company." He highlighted the sale of the Professional division, elimination of all outstanding debt, a $2 per share special dividend, and a $5 million share buyback. "As a result of those actions, today, we are a solely focused property management staffing organization, debt-free with a strong cash position."
  • Schroeder explained, "we utilized the findings from an independent consulting firm to help shape our top line revenue initiatives as we finalize our budget for 2026 and beyond."
  • Schroeder stated, "we continue to take aggressive actions to resize our general and administrative expenses to be more in line with our stand-alone property staffing business," estimating ongoing G&A costs at $12 million, with $2 million in public company costs.
  • Schroeder also noted, "we are utilizing results of an external organizational and incentive compensation study to take further actions to reduce selling and G&A costs, primarily in the selling cost area," with annualized cost savings of approximately $1 million expected to benefit results starting in Q3 2026.
  • Kelly Brown, Co-CEO and President of Property Management, announced a rebranding: "At the completion of our TSA agreement in April, we will transition our website to bgstaffing.com. Our analysis... proved that including staffing in our name consistently ranks us in the top 3 results for both clients seeking talent and job seekers." Brown emphasized the launch into the PropTech support market, stating, "In February, we announced our first software partnership with Yardi, an industry-leading property management technology platform."
  • Brown shared that technology and AI investments "have improved front and back-office efficiency, while reinforcing our people-first culture."
  • Schroeder concluded, "Fourth quarter revenues were $22 million, a 9.4% decline compared to the prior year, driven by lower billed hours and weak demand due to overall cost pressures on property management companies and property owners."

Outlook

  • Schroeder indicated, "we do expect full year sales in 2026 to be over 2025 kind of in the mid-single digits."
  • Brown explained, "we are executing on our 2026 top line strategic initiatives, leveraging insights from the market study completed late last year."
  • Both executives projected that revenue growth should occur in every quarter of 2026, driven by improved market sentiment and internal execution.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenues were $22 million, a 9.4% decline from the prior year.
  • Gross profit for the quarter was $7.7 million, with gross profit as a percentage of revenue at 35%.
  • Adjusted for $147,000 in out-of-period workers’ comp costs, gross profit as a percentage of revenue was 35.6%.
  • SG&A expenses for the quarter were $9.3 million, including $403,000 in strategic review costs and approximately $460,000 of out-of-period expenses.
  • Fourth quarter adjusted EBITDA was a loss of $947,000, compared to an EBITDA loss of $1.6 million in the prior year.
  • GAAP net loss from continuing operations was $0.11 per diluted share; non-GAAP adjusted EPS loss from continuing operations was $0.09 per share; consolidated adjusted non-GAAP EPS for the quarter was $0.09 per share.
  • Full-year net cash provided by continuing operating activities was $117,000, with capital expenditures at $138,000.
  • The company purchased 351,200 shares during 2025, totaling approximately $1.5 million, with total purchases to date of 522,000 shares at $2.4 million.

Q&A

  • William Dezellem, Tieton Capital Management, LLC: Asked about the Yardi relationship, its business model, and long-term implications. Brown responded that "Yardi as a company has established an independent consultant network" and BGSF will fill gaps in software implementation and training for property management customers. "So Yardi essentially serves as a referral base... and it's a really basic model of hiring the consultant, placing them and then billing accordingly."
  • Dezellem asked about the size of the Yardi opportunity. Brown replied, "the potential is very large across all of our customer base," with opportunities at the corporate office level and end-user level.
  • Dezellem inquired about SG&A details. Schroeder explained, "G&A costs that we are estimating going forward once we're clear the TSA... is around $12 million." He clarified that Q4 costs were higher due to transition-related expenses.
  • Dezellem asked about the overall market environment. Brown said, "This year is much more optimistic of a sentiment as what we were experiencing last year."
  • Dezellem probed about deferred maintenance and potential catch-up spending. Brown responded, "there may be a little bit of that," but most customers leverage existing employees rather than increasing spending immediately.
  • Schroeder added, "our top line sales through the first 2 months are slightly ahead of 2025. So it's been off to a solid start for this year."
  • Dezellem requested specifics on monthly trends. Schroeder stated, "as we ended 2025, the seasonality effects that we would expect, we were better than those in the last month of last year."
  • George Melas, MKH Management Company, LLC: Sought clarification on PropTech as a new business segment. Brown confirmed, "Yes, it is different from the type of staffing that we've delivered in the past," with an initial pool of 8 to 12 consultants and first-year top line projections of $1 million to $2 million.
  • Melas asked about training for Yardi consultants. Brown said, "They tend to come in with existing Yardi experience... Yardi does provide really impressive resources to make sure their consultant base has access to training and knowledge."
  • Melas asked about geographic expansion. Brown responded, "this service is not necessarily geographically driven because a lot of the work... is remote."
  • Melas questioned the drivers behind the improved sales trend. Schroeder attributed it mainly to internal execution improvements: "The things that we learned from one of the studies is the speed to fill, getting the right candidate in the right spot quickly."
  • Melas asked about cost structure. Schroeder confirmed, "G&A is pretty flat," and ongoing efforts are in place to further reduce costs.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, with a focus on market recovery, cost controls, and the new PropTech initiative. Questions were probing but constructive, especially on new business lines and cost savings.
  • Management’s tone was confident, frequently referencing execution and strategic progress. Phrases like "we are laser-focused on that stuff" and "we do expect full year sales in 2026 to be over 2025 kind of in the mid-single digits" signaled increased confidence versus prior quarters.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's sentiment shifted from transitional caution to a more forward-looking, confident outlook, reinforced by strategic execution and early signs of sales recovery.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management’s language evolved from focusing on transition and restructuring to emphasizing execution, cost controls, and new growth initiatives.
  • Guidance shifted from general growth expectations to a specific projection of mid-single-digit revenue growth in 2026.
  • Analysts’ questions moved from clarifying restructuring and market studies to digging into execution, new business segments, and quantifying near-term opportunities.
  • The introduction of PropTech and the Yardi partnership marks a new strategic direction compared to the previous quarter.
  • Management’s tone displayed more confidence and specificity in cost control targets and growth plans.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited continued cost pressures on property owners and property management companies as a driver for revenue softness.
  • One-time and out-of-period expenses impacted reported margins and SG&A.
  • Brown acknowledged that PropTech revenue projections are early and will be monitored closely as the initiative ramps up.
  • Analysts were attentive to execution risk, sustainability of cost reductions, and the scalability of new business segments.

Final Takeaway

BGSF completed a major strategic transition to a focused property management staffing platform, supported by aggressive cost reduction and the launch of a new PropTech consulting business with Yardi. Management projects mid-single-digit revenue growth in 2026, driven by improved execution and market sentiment, while maintaining a disciplined approach to expenses and capital allocation. The company’s debt-free status, strong cash position, and technology investments position it to capitalize on evolving customer needs and adjacent market opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BWX Technologies started Buy at TD Cowen on 'unmatched nuclear heritage'
data.description:
BWX Technologies (BWXT) +1.2% in Thursday's trading as TD Cowen initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $230 price target, seeing the company's "strong nuclear heritage and capabilities combined with being a technology-agnostic service and equipment provider as very attractive."

The company has developed a portfolio of nuclear related businesses across government and commercial operations that not only provides strong recurring cash flow but also presents an opportunity for significant growth that could translate to more than $10B of value, TD Cowen analyst Marc Bianchi said, seeing further upside potential even while some of the value upside may have already been reflected in the stock.

BWXT (BWXT) is technology agnostic and has the only large component manufacturing capacity in North America that is nuclear certified, Bianchi noted, adding the company has been selected to reestablish domestic uranium enrichment for defense purposes, which could ultimately become a recurring business to replace the enriched uranium stockpile.

The analyst sees potential near-term catalysts including progress on the Trump administration's goal to build 10 new reactors by 2030; formal plans to expand capacity at the company's Mount Vernon, Indiana, site; and an updated 30-year shipbuilding plan expected soon from the U.S. Navy.
